title: Where Rei and Gendo Eat Together in 20
display_title: Where Rei and Gendo Eat Together in 20
wikitext:
[]: Where Rei and Gendo Eat Together in 2.0             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jun 24, 2013 3:16 am ElMariachi wrote: Maybe the piano was in the NERV recreation room(didn't they mention something like that in "Magma Diver" in NGE?), or in the restaurant where Gendo was eating with Rei(we see other tables, so it's not for Gendo's exclusive use), or maybe there is a reception hall and a piano is put there. For a base as big as NERV HQ, which double as the Main Branch and "heart" of the whole organization, the existence of such facilities wouldn't be that far fetched.

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Mon Jun 24, 2013 3:32 am Hypothetically Gendo could've just had the entire restaurant to himself, so he could be alone with Rei, and Fuyutsuki also.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Jun 24, 2013 3:35 am Maybe it's like a VIP only lounge/restaurant were Gendo would entertain special guests that are already as deeply involved as he is, that would explain why the markings are still present. Heck maybe Kaworu even made an appearance there and took a liking to the piano and requested it be moved.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jun 24, 2013 3:38 am In terms of visual language, I don't think those markings would be there except to signal to us that this is in fact Gendo's office. That's certainly what was communicated to me. From a narrative logic standpoint, why the heck put similar-looking markings on a completely unrelated room?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jun 24, 2013 3:55 am There is only one door to enter Gendo's office, how would the staff make enter all these tables in the office? Besides Gendo always have been an extremely practical and stark man, only accommodating with the strict minimum, I really don't see him requesting such a fancy and complicated process of installing a whole restaurant in his office twice per day just for him and sometimes Rei to eat, that would be an unnecessary waste of time and resources! My theory is that the "restaurant" is in the same level than Gendo's office : in the NERV pyramid, you can see an horizontal line a little bellow the tip of the pyramid, that's actually windows, and Gendo's personal office is situated here but only take a part of the floor, so there is probably other room for the Commander(and maybe Vice-Commander too) use, this high-class personal restaurant room being on of these rooms, the presence of others table meaning that this room also serves as a reception hall when important personalities come. And to encompass that we're still in "Gendo's personal space"(i.e. the Commander floor), the floor and ceiling have the same kabbalistic marking than his office.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Jun 24, 2013 4:02 am I'm not so sure about that being Gendo's office. True in the Eva tv series the tree of life did indicate that, however the setup of that room in 2.0 doesn't scream Gendo to me, it's a bit too relaxed. The markings this time could indicate something different like a unified goal.(That he shares with Seele at that point ) That along with lighter atmosphere makes me think that's not his office. Though that might just be the music

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Jun 24, 2013 7:40 am ElMariachi wrote: Besides Gendo always have been an extremely practical and stark man, only accommodating with the strict minimum, I really don't see him requesting such a fancy and complicated process of installing a whole restaurant in his office twice per day just for him and sometimes Rei to eat, that would be an unnecessary waste of time and resources!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jun 24, 2013 8:12 am That's why I'm saying that this restaurant must also serve as a reception hall when important members of NERV, the UN or politicians(ambassadors, Japanese government...) come to NERV. By the way did the script of the CRC says something about the room were Rei and Gendo are eating?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jun 24, 2013 9:24 am The patterns seen in the restaurant are different to the ones seen in Gendo's office. Expensive floor designs  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Mon Jun 24, 2013 9:42 am Hmmmm.....The dining room either is located in lower/higher level of the pyramid or in the "pool."

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jun 24, 2013 1:24 pm My guess is lower level; the windows are longer, meaning that there has to be more space for them to be built. The lower you go, the more space there is.

[]:             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Tue Jun 25, 2013 2:31 pm I am getting a definate "VIP Lounge" or "Dining/Ballroom" like you see in some hotels. An outside restaurant would be extremely unlikely. Occam's Razor says that it's most likely so similar because they reused as much of an already stark room's film as possible and laid additional cells and color changes overtop of it to reduce costs of drawing and animating a new area.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jun 25, 2013 6:49 pm Welp, OMF has convinced me with that side-by-side comparison.
